Use order preview methods (execute nothing) placing live orders. trading examples run public market-data host need auth; order examples require credentials illustrative .

title: Asynchronous Usage with coinbase
text: Synchronous HTTP blocks R session waiting reply. Asynchronous mode lets fire multiple requests process results arrive — useful bots poll several products place orders parallel. async branch point covers every class: CoinbaseMarketData, CoinbaseAccount, CoinbaseTrading, CoinbaseFutures.

title: Asynchronous Usage with coinbase
text: Public market data needs credentials. authenticated classes, store CDP API key .Renviron (PEM newlines escaped \n single line) let get_api_keys() read — never hardcode secrets: Event loop: R built-event loop like Node.js Python’s asyncio. Promises resolve event loop ticks via later::run_now(). scripts vignettes, drain loop (!loop_empty()) run_now(). Shiny apps event loop runs automatically.
code:
box::use(   coinbase[CoinbaseMarketData, CoinbaseTrading, CoinbaseAccount, get_api_keys],   coro[async, await],   later[run_now, loop_empty],   promises[then, catch, promise_all] ) COINBASE_API_KEY_NAME="organizations/<org-uuid>/apiKeys/<key-uuid>" COINBASE_API_PRIVATE_KEY="-----BEGIN EC PRIVATE KEY-----
...
-----END EC PRIVATE KEY-----
" keys <- get_api_keys()

title: Asynchronous Usage with coinbase
text: ergonomic way work promises R coro::async, lets write code looks synchronous runs asynchronously hood — just like TypeScript’s async/await. Pass async = TRUE class constructor; methods return promises instead data.tables: Key pattern: define async function, await API call, capture result <<-, drive event loop (!loop_empty()) run_now(). resolved value single-row data.table numeric ask, bid, price, size, volume, POSIXct time.
code: market <- CoinbaseMarketData$new(async = TRUE) ticker <- NULL  get_ticker <- async(function() {   res <- await(market$get_ticker("BTC-USD"))   ticker <<- res   return(invisible(NULL)) })  get_ticker() while (!loop_empty()) {   run_now() } ticker #>         ask      bid   volume   trade_id    price    size                time #>       <num>    <num>    <num>      <int>    <num>   <num>              <POSc> #> 1: 74101.53 74101.52 3600.231 1026942323 74101.53 5.2e-07 2026-05-31 04:58:29 #>    rfq_volume #>         <num> #> 1:    10.7929

title: Asynchronous Usage with coinbase
text: Chain several awaited calls sequence — one resolves next begins, just like await TypeScript: ohlcv element data.table columns datetime, open, high, low, close, volume.
code: market <- CoinbaseMarketData$new(async = TRUE) results <- NULL  fetch_data <- async(function() {   ticker <- await(market$get_ticker("BTC-USD"))   ohlcv <- await(market$get_ohlcv("BTC-USD", granularity = "1min"))   results <<- list(ticker = ticker, ohlcv = ohlcv)   return(invisible(NULL)) })  fetch_data() while (!loop_empty()) {   run_now() } results$ticker results$ohlcv #>         ask      bid   volume   trade_id    price    size                time #>       <num>    <num>    <num>      <int>    <num>   <num>              <POSc> #> 1: 74101.53 74101.52 3600.231 1026942323 74101.53 5.2e-07 2026-05-31 04:58:29 #>    rfq_volume #>         <num> #> 1:    10.7929 #>               datetime     open     high      low    close volume #>                 <POSc>    <num>    <num>    <num>    <num>  <num> #> 1: 2026-05-31 04:53:00 74055.40 74070.12 74050.00 74067.15 1.4820 #> 2: 2026-05-31 04:54:00 74113.49 74113.49 74067.15 74068.26 3.0535 #> 3: 2026-05-31 04:55:00 74068.26 74093.61 74068.26 74093.60 0.9691 #> 4: 2026-05-31 04:56:00 74093.60 74099.83 74093.59 74099.83 0.1151

title: Asynchronous Usage with coinbase
text: requests independent, fire simultaneously collect results — async equivalent Promise.() TypeScript:
code: market <- CoinbaseMarketData$new(async = TRUE) results <- NULL  fetch_parallel <- async(function() {   # Launch both requests concurrently — no await yet, just collect promises   ticker_promise <- market$get_ticker("BTC-USD")   candles_promise <- market$get_ohlcv("BTC-USD", granularity = "1hour")   # Await them together — like Promise.all([ticker, candles])   res <- await(promise_all(ticker = ticker_promise, candles = candles_promise))   results <<- res   return(invisible(NULL)) })  fetch_parallel() while (!loop_empty()) {   run_now() } results$ticker results$candles #>         ask      bid   volume   trade_id    price    size                time #>       <num>    <num>    <num>      <int>    <num>   <num>              <POSc> #> 1: 74101.53 74101.52 3600.231 1026942323 74101.53 5.2e-07 2026-05-31 04:58:29 #>    rfq_volume #>         <num> #> 1:    10.7929 #>               datetime     open     high      low    close volume #>                 <POSc>    <num>    <num>    <num>    <num>  <num> #> 1: 2026-05-31 04:53:00 74055.40 74070.12 74050.00 74067.15 1.4820 #> 2: 2026-05-31 04:54:00 74113.49 74113.49 74067.15 74068.26 3.0535 #> 3: 2026-05-31 04:55:00 74068.26 74093.61 74068.26 74093.60 0.9691 #> 4: 2026-05-31 04:56:00 74093.60 74099.83 74093.59 74099.83 0.1151

title: Asynchronous Usage with coinbase
text: common use case gathering several views product — ticker, recent candles, recent trades, order book. async mode, requests fly parallel rather sequentially: lapply + .call(promise_all, ...) shape scales watchlist: build one promise per product, name list product id, await together.
code: market <- CoinbaseMarketData$new(async = TRUE) snapshot <- NULL  fetch_snapshot <- async(function() {   # Fire all requests concurrently   promises <- list(     ticker = market$get_ticker("BTC-USD"),     candles = market$get_ohlcv("BTC-USD", granularity = "1min"),     trades = market$get_trades("BTC-USD", limit = 100),     orderbook = market$get_orderbook("BTC-USD", level = 2)   )    # Await all at once   res <- await(do.call(promise_all, promises))   snapshot <<- res   return(invisible(NULL)) })  fetch_snapshot() while (!loop_empty()) {   run_now() }  # Each element is the parsed data.table for one endpoint snapshot$ticker snapshot$orderbook #>         ask      bid   volume   trade_id    price    size                time #>       <num>    <num>    <num>      <int>    <num>   <num>              <POSc> #> 1: 74101.53 74101.52 3600.231 1026942323 74101.53 5.2e-07 2026-05-31 04:58:29 #>    rfq_volume #>         <num> #> 1:    10.7929 #>      side    price       size num_orders #>    <char>    <num>      <num>      <num> #> 1:    bid 74101.52 0.43541938          5 #> 2:    bid 74101.38 0.00067474          1 #> 3:    bid 74098.69 0.00266800          1 #> 4:    ask 74101.53 0.08631688          7 #> 5:    ask 74103.63 0.26058806          2 #> 6:    ask 74103.66 0.00140588          2

title: Asynchronous Usage with coinbase
text: prefer promise-pipeline style (common JavaScript), resolve promise handle rejection catch. alternative coro::async/await:
code: market <- CoinbaseMarketData$new(async = TRUE) chain_result <- NULL  stats_promise <- market$get_ohlcv("BTC-USD", granularity = "1hour") handled <- then(stats_promise, function(ohlcv) {   chain_result <<- ohlcv   return(invisible(NULL)) }) catch(handled, function(err) {   message("Error: ", conditionMessage(err))   return(invisible(NULL)) })  while (!loop_empty()) {   run_now() } chain_result #>               datetime     open     high      low    close volume #>                 <POSc>    <num>    <num>    <num>    <num>  <num> #> 1: 2026-05-31 04:53:00 74055.40 74070.12 74050.00 74067.15 1.4820 #> 2: 2026-05-31 04:54:00 74113.49 74113.49 74067.15 74068.26 3.0535 #> 3: 2026-05-31 04:55:00 74068.26 74093.61 74068.26 74093.60 0.9691 #> 4: 2026-05-31 04:56:00 74093.60 74099.83 74093.59 74099.83 0.1151

title: Asynchronous Usage with coinbase
text: Trading methods return promises async mode just like market data. Always preview order (dry run places nothing) submitting live one. Sequential await keeps flow readable. Construct client credentials (read .Renviron via get_api_keys()): place live order, call add_order() instead preview_order(). takes product_id, side, order_configuration, plus optional client_order_id, self_trade_prevention_id, leverage, margin_type, retail_portfolio_id; resolves single-row data.table success, scalar order_id, product_id, side, client_order_id, failure_reason.
code: trading <- CoinbaseTrading$new(async = TRUE) results <- NULL  preview_then_query <- async(function() {   # Dry run — executes nothing; validates the configuration   preview <- await(trading$preview_order(     "BTC-USD",     "BUY",     list(market_market_ioc = list(quote_size = "10"))   ))    # Query recent orders for this product   orders <- await(trading$get_orders(product_ids = "BTC-USD", limit = 10))    results <<- list(preview = preview, orders = orders)   return(invisible(NULL)) })  preview_then_query() while (!loop_empty()) {   run_now() } results$preview[, .(order_total, commission_total, base_size, best_ask, errs)] results$orders[, .(order_id, product_id, side, status, order_type, filled_size)] #>    order_total commission_total base_size best_ask   errs #>          <num>            <num>     <num>    <num> <char> #> 1:       10.06             0.06  0.000135 74101.53   <NA> #>                                order_id product_id   side status order_type #>                                  <char>     <char> <char> <char>     <char> #> 1: 1111aaaa-2222-bbbb-3333-cccccccccccc    BTC-USD    BUY   OPEN      LIMIT #> 2: 4444dddd-5555-eeee-6666-ffffffffffff    ETH-USD   SELL FILLED     MARKET #>    filled_size #>          <num> #> 1:         0.0 #> 2:         0.5

title: Asynchronous Usage with coinbase
text: Inside async functions, use tryCatch around await calls structured error handling — await() re-throws promise rejection normal R error, try/catch pattern works directly. malformed product id rejected (validated request sent) caught:
code: market <- CoinbaseMarketData$new(async = TRUE) result <- NULL  safe_fetch <- async(function() {   res <- tryCatch(     await(market$get_ticker("NOTAVALIDSYMBOL")),     error = function(e) {       message("Caught error: ", conditionMessage(e))       return(NULL)     }   )   result <<- res   return(invisible(NULL)) })  safe_fetch() while (!loop_empty()) {   run_now() }

title: Asynchronous Usage with coinbase
text: critical piece async R event loop. Promises resolve event loop ticks. interactive session Shiny app, event loop runs automatically. scripts vignettes, must drive manually. timeout guard: Shiny applications, event loop managed — simply return promises reactive expressions Shiny handles resolution.
code: # Idiomatic event loop drain while (!later::loop_empty()) {   later::run_now() } deadline <- lubridate::now() + lubridate::seconds(30) # 30-second timeout while (!later::loop_empty() && lubridate::now() < deadline) {   later::run_now(timeoutSecs = 0.1) }

title: Asynchronous Usage with coinbase
text: Rule thumb: await() must appear RHS <- bare statement — never inside another expression. Assign result local <-, copy async body <<- await returns.

title: Asynchronous Usage with coinbase
text: See vignette("getting-started") tour package synchronous mode. Review CoinbaseMarketData, CoinbaseAccount, CoinbaseTrading, CoinbaseFutures class documentation full method coverage. Explore Coinbase Advanced Trade API documentation endpoint details.

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: Four R6 classes plus handful standalone helpers wrap Coinbase Advanced Trade API (authenticated trading / account / futures) public Coinbase Exchange API (market data). Every class supports synchronous asynchronous (promise-based) operation via httr2, selected async flag construction. four inherit common abstract base (CoinbaseBase) handles JWT signing, sync/async dispatch, body-cursor pagination. Standalone (class):

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: Coinbase Advanced Trade uses CDP key-pair credentials (key name plus privateKey), API key / secret pair. Download JSON https://www.coinbase.com/settings/api store two fields .Renviron. multi-line privateKey escaped onto one line replacing real newlines two characters \n; get_api_keys() unescapes back signing. placeholders deliberately fake; never commit real key. EC keys (ES256) base64 Ed25519 keys (EdDSA) supported signer. R, using box::use rather library(). environment variables set, get_api_keys() reads arguments:
code:
COINBASE_API_KEY_NAME=organizations/<org-uuid>/apiKeys/<key-uuid> COINBASE_API_PRIVATE_KEY=-----BEGIN EC PRIVATE KEY-----
<...base64...>
-----END EC PRIVATE KEY-----
 box::use(   coinbase[     CoinbaseMarketData, CoinbaseAccount, CoinbaseTrading,     CoinbaseFutures, get_api_keys   ] )  keys    <- get_api_keys()                 # reads .Renviron market  <- CoinbaseMarketData$new()       # public, no keys needed account <- CoinbaseAccount$new(keys = keys) trading <- CoinbaseTrading$new(keys = keys) futures <- CoinbaseFutures$new(keys = keys)

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions

Public market data needs keys:

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: get_ohlcv() (parser parse_candles) reorders Coinbase’s raw [time, low, high, open, close, volume] array canonical layout sorts ascending. Exactly six columns: granularity one "1min", "5min", "15min", "1hour", "6hour", "1day".

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: get_trades() get_trades_history() (parser parse_trades) return one row per tick trade, exactly five typed columns: page deeper history get_trades(), pass smallest trade_id seen far ; get_trades_history() loop returns single de-duplicated, ascending table:

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: get_orderbook() (parser parse_orderbook) one long-format market-data method: bids asks arrays stacked one table side column ("bid" / "ask"). shape third column depends requested level: levels 1 2 book aggregated, entry’s third element order count (num_orders). level 3 book full non-aggregated book, third element order_id string; parser emits character order_id column instead coercing numeric. given call returns one , never . level 3 third column character order_id instead num_orders (single call returns one , never ):
code: # Top 5 highest-priced asks (aggregated level-2 book) depth <- market$get_orderbook("BTC-USD", level = 2L) depth[side == "ask"][order(-price)][1:min(5, .N)] #>      side    price       size num_orders #>    <char>    <num>      <num>      <num> #> 1:    ask 74103.66 0.00140588          2 #> 2:    ask 74103.63 0.26058806          2 #> 3:    ask 74101.53 0.08631688          7 l3 <- market$get_orderbook("BTC-USD", level = 3L) l3[, .(side, price, size, order_id)] #>      side    price       size                             order_id #>    <char>    <num>      <num>                               <char> #> 1:    bid 74101.52 0.21000000 b1a2c3d4-0001-4aaa-8bbb-000000000001 #> 2:    bid 74101.52 0.22541938 b1a2c3d4-0002-4aaa-8bbb-000000000002 #> 3:    bid 74098.69 0.00266800 b1a2c3d4-0003-4aaa-8bbb-000000000003 #> 4:    ask 74101.53 0.04000000 a1a2c3d4-0001-4aaa-8bbb-000000000001 #> 5:    ask 74101.53 0.04631688 a1a2c3d4-0002-4aaa-8bbb-000000000002 #> 6:    ask 74103.66 0.00140588 a1a2c3d4-0003-4aaa-8bbb-000000000003

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: endpoints require credentials. get_orders() get_fills() walk body-cursor pagination. Orders carry order_configuration — one-key list naming detailed order type (e.g. market_market_ioc, limit_limit_gtc, stop_limit_stop_limit_gtc) — parsers flatten scalar columns (see Treatment C ). Note recently-widened signatures: add_order() / preview_order() take leverage, margin_type ("CROSS" / "ISOLATED"), retail_portfolio_id (plus self_trade_prevention_id add_order ); get_orders() takes full filter set ; get_fills() filters order_ids (plural) trade_ids, sequence-timestamp bounds sort_by.
code: trading <- CoinbaseTrading$new()

text: deep-history path. Coinbase’s /candles endpoint shallow, complete OHLCV timeframe built ticks: download trades, aggregate. trades_to_ohlcv() takes data.table time (POSIXct), price (numeric), size (numeric) columns — exactly get_trades() / get_trades_history() return — plus interval seconds, returns canonical six-column OHLCV table: Open/close first/last trade time (trade_id breaks ties within identical timestamp present); empty intervals produce row. column layout matches get_ohlcv() exactly, candle data either source interchangeable.

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: Now ’ve seen surface, rules used decide row returned data.table represents. Every method follows one principle: Identify entity endpoint, return one row per entity. trade gets row. order gets row. account balance gets row. candle gets row. Anything nested entity becomes flat column row additional row different axis — never list column. overriding invariant coinbase -list-column contract. Even generic flatteners (as_dt_row / as_dt_list) enforce : nested object, array, multi-element value doesn’t hand-written parser collapsed single JSON string rather left list cell. four treatments.

what: Treatment A — Flatten {value, currency} amounts to numerics
title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: Coinbase represents monetary amounts nested {value, currency} objects wire. Every field flattened numeric value (helpers amount_value / flex_num), dropping currency tag — currency already implied column sibling currency column. ’s used: available_balance / hold accounts; every amount futures balance summary, positions, sweeps (futures_buying_power, unrealized_pnl, current_price, avg_entry_price, requested_amount, …). flex_num lenient variant — accepts either bare scalar {value, currency} object — futures fields arrive either form depending account state.
code: accts <- account$get_accounts() accts$available_balance     # numeric, not list({value=..., currency=...})  bal <- futures$get_balance_summary() bal$futures_buying_power    # numeric, flattened from {value, currency} #> [1]     0.5321 12500.4200 #> [1] 9500

what: Treatment B — Long format for arrays of objects
title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: array’s elements records (price, size, side, …), explode one row per element side position column order matters. coinbase applies exactly one endpoint: order book. bids asks arrays stacked side discriminator. level-3 distinction subtle part: levels 1-2 third element order count (num_orders, numeric); level 3 book non-aggregated third element order_id string, parser emits character order_id column instead. Check got "order_id" %% names(ob).

what: Treatment C — Flatten fixed-schema nested objects to scalar columns
title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: nested data object (array) known fixed key set, flatten inner fields scalar columns row. entity stays one row. ’s used: fee_tier get_fees (pricing_tier / maker_fee_rate / taker_fee_rate / usd_from / usd_to); order_configuration orders, create-order, preview (config_type, base_size, quote_size, limit_price, stop_price, stop_trigger_price, stop_direction, end_time, post_only — see flatten_order_config); margin_window get_current_margin_window. detailed-type key order_configuration surfaced config_type column, distinct coarse order_type enum.
code: fees <- account$get_fees() fees$maker_fee_rate   # was nested under fee_tier fees$pricing_tier     # was nested under fee_tier  orders <- trading$get_orders(product_ids = "BTC-USD") orders$config_type    # the order_configuration's one key orders$limit_price    # was nested inside order_configuration #> [1] 0.004 #> [1] "Advanced 1" #> [1] "limit_limit_gtc"   "market_market_ioc" #> [1] 70000    NA

what: Treatment D — Collapse error arrays to one string
title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: Coinbase returns validation / preview / edit errors array whose elements sometimes objects sometimes bare strings, whose reason lives different keys per endpoint (error_code, new_order_failure_reason, preview_failure_reason, edit_failure_reason, failure_reason, error, message, …). Rather leave list column, collapse_errors looks element known keys joins array single "; "-separated character string — NA_character_ errors. ’s used: errs preview_order; failure_reason add_order cancel_orders; errors edit_order preview_edit_order.
code: pv <- trading$preview_order(   "BTC-USD", "BUY",   list(market_market_ioc = list(quote_size = "10")) ) pv$errs   # one string, or NA when the preview validated cleanly #> [1] NA

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: Methods without hand-written parser (get_products, get_product, get_ticker, get_portfolios, get_key_permissions, get_server_time, schedule_sweep, cancel_sweep, get_intraday_margin_setting) go as_dt_row / as_dt_list. flatten every scalar field column , crucially, collapse unexpected nested object/array multi-element value single JSON string — even endpoint package doesn’t model detail still returns list-column-free table. Recover field jsonlite::fromJSON() ever need structure.

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: apply every treatment, regardless method. Empty / null field → scalar NA (typed), list cell. missing balance NA_real_, missing timestamp NA (POSIXct), missing string NA_character_. Downstream .na() keeps working; list-column gymnastics. Empty response → empty data.table. synthetic stub rows. get_trades() nothing return, cancel_orders() whose batch matched nothing, get_positions() open positions — return zero-row table. nrow() honest count “things got back,” absence error success signal.

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: intentional non-goals, shared sister alpaca, binance, kucoin packages: list columns, ever. headline guarantee. Nested {value, currency} amounts flattened numerics, fixed-schema objects scalar columns, error arrays single string, unmodelled nesting JSON string. returned table never hides list cell. automatic local-time conversion. Timestamps come back UTC POSIXct. Convert format(x, tz = "America/New_York") similar display time. client_order_id round-tripped verbatim. Whatever string pass (UUID generate_client_order_id() mints) goes back unchanged. client-side rate-limiting. package surfaces Coinbase’s rate-limit errors back — caller’s job. reconnect / retry transient network errors. single call asked ; wrap httr2::req_retry() (passed coinbase_build_request()) want retries.

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: alpaca, binance, kucoin, coinbase packages follow shape rule. ’ve learned data.table idioms one , idioms work others — pivot portfolio’s balances across multiple exchanges straight rbindlist plus exchange column, per-source shape massage escape hatch lapply hidden lists. predictability makes connector layer (exchanges package, top tradebot-core) easy write: raw wrapper returns data connector can re-shape without special-casing list columns unwrapping nested structures.
code: # Combine spot accounts + futures balance into one ledger ledger <- data.table::rbindlist(   list(     account$get_accounts()[, wallet := "spot"],     futures$get_balance_summary()[, wallet := "futures"]   ),   use.names = TRUE,   fill = TRUE ) ledger[, .(wallet, currency, available_balance, futures_buying_power)] #>     wallet currency available_balance futures_buying_power #>     <char>   <char>             <num>                <num> #> 1:    spot      BTC            0.5321                   NA #> 2:    spot      USD        12500.4200                   NA #> 3: futures     <NA>                NA                 9500

title: Shorting with US Futures (CFM) with coinbase
text: US customers trade CFM futures covered package. Coinbase’s INTX perpetual-futures endpoints eligible non-US jurisdictions wrapped. open short, submit SELL order CFM futures product (expiring contract nano-BTC future); CoinbaseFutures reports resulting position, margin, PnL. complements alpaca package, trades long US equities: holding long stocks alpaca shorting correlated crypto future CFM building block pairs / statistical-arbitrage strategy spanning two brokers.

title: Shorting with US Futures (CFM) with coinbase
text: Credentials come Coinbase Developer Platform (JSON file name privateKey). Store .Renviron COINBASE_API_KEY_NAME COINBASE_API_PRIVATE_KEY; multi-line privateKey placed one line newlines escaped two characters \n, get_api_keys() unescapes signing. CFM futures account also requires separate approval funding Coinbase. set, get_api_keys() reads arguments, simply construct client credentials picked automatically. Public market data needs credentials .
code:
# In ~/.Renviron (these are obviously-fake placeholders — never commit real keys): COINBASE_API_KEY_NAME="organizations/<org-uuid>/apiKeys/<key-uuid>" COINBASE_API_PRIVATE_KEY="-----BEGIN EC PRIVATE KEY-----
<fake-key-body>
-----END EC PRIVATE KEY-----
" box::use(   coinbase[     CoinbaseTrading, CoinbaseFutures, CoinbaseMarketData, get_api_keys   ] )  keys <- get_api_keys()  market <- CoinbaseMarketData$new() trading <- CoinbaseTrading$new(keys = keys) futures <- CoinbaseFutures$new(keys = keys)

text: Read set intraday margin setting. Intraday margin lowers margin requirement trading session (raising effective leverage, risk); standard setting applies full requirement. title: Shorting with US Futures (CFM) with coinbase
text: common use short leg market-neutral pair: hold long US-equity position alpaca short correlated crypto future CFM. two brokers driven independently — alpaca long stock leg, coinbase short futures leg — reports positions PnL: Monitor legs’ exposure: equity position via alpaca’s account methods, futures short via futures$get_positions() futures$get_balance_summary().
code: # Long leg (alpaca): buy the equity # alpaca_trading$add_order(symbol = "COIN", side = "buy", type = "market", #   time_in_force = "day", qty = 100)  # Short leg (coinbase CFM): preview the offsetting futures short trading$preview_order(   product_id = "BIT-31OCT26-CDE",   side = "SELL",   order_configuration = list(market_market_ioc = list(base_size = "1")) )[, .(order_total, commission_total, base_size, errs)] #>    order_total commission_total base_size   errs #>          <num>            <num>     <num> <char> #> 1:       10.06             0.06  0.000135   <NA>

title: Shorting with US Futures (CFM) with coinbase
text: Every method runs asynchronously client constructed async = TRUE, returning promise. Consume promises coro::async() await(), drain event loop — without pipe operator:
code: box::use(coro[async, await], later)  futures_async <- CoinbaseFutures$new(keys = keys, async = TRUE) run <- async(function() {   summary <- await(futures_async$get_balance_summary())   positions <- await(futures_async$get_positions())   print(summary[, .(futures_buying_power, available_margin, liquidation_threshold)])   print(positions[, .(product_id, side, number_of_contracts, unrealized_pnl)])   return(invisible(NULL)) })  run()  # Drive the event loop until the promise settles while (!later$loop_empty()) {   later$run_now() } #>    futures_buying_power available_margin liquidation_threshold #>                   <num>            <num>                 <num> #> 1:                 9500             7260                   370 #>         product_id   side number_of_contracts unrealized_pnl #>             <char> <char>               <num>          <num> #> 1: BIT-31OCT26-CDE  SHORT                   3         -125.4

title: Getting Started with coinbase
text: Public market data needs credentials. Authenticated endpoints (accounts, fees, trading) require Coinbase Developer Platform (CDP) API key. Create one https://www.coinbase.com/settings/api download JSON file, contains name privateKey. Store credentials environment variables .Renviron. downloaded privateKey multi-line PEM; keep single line, escape newlines two characters \n (get_api_keys() unescapes back use). EC keys (-----BEGIN EC PRIVATE KEY-----, signed ES256) base64-encoded Ed25519 keys (signed EdDSA) supported. Two hosts. Coinbase splits across two hosts. Authenticated trading account endpoints use Advanced Trade host (https://api.coinbase.com, get_base_url()); public market-data endpoints deep history use Exchange host (https://api.exchange.coinbase.com, get_exchange_base_url()). class selects correct host per request, rarely set hand. environment variables set, get_api_keys() reads arguments, practice simply call CoinbaseAccount$new() credentials picked automatically.
code:
COINBASE_API_KEY_NAME="organizations/<org-uuid>/apiKeys/<key-uuid>" COINBASE_API_PRIVATE_KEY="-----BEGIN EC PRIVATE KEY-----
<fake-key-body>
-----END EC PRIVATE KEY-----
" box::use(   coinbase[     CoinbaseMarketData, CoinbaseAccount, CoinbaseTrading,     trades_to_ohlcv, coinbase_backfill_trades, get_api_keys   ] )  keys <- get_api_keys(   api_key_name = "organizations/<org-uuid>/apiKeys/<key-uuid>",   api_private_key = "-----BEGIN EC PRIVATE KEY-----
<fake-key-body>
-----END EC PRIVATE KEY-----
" )

text: Coinbase’s candle endpoint shallow, complete OHLCV timeframe built ticks. get_trades_history() pages trades endpoint backwards recent trade toward start, deduplicates, returns trades sorted ascending time: Note: Tick volume large. Aggregate ticks OHLCV bars interval (seconds) trades_to_ohlcv(). result mirrors get_ohlcv(): datetime, open, high, low, close, volume:

title: Getting Started with coinbase
text: coinbase_backfill_trades() data-collection workflow: downloads deep tick history one products writes results CSV incrementally, progress survives interruption. Re-running call resumes product last recorded trade. writes file disk, shown executed: function returns file path invisibly. symbols failed, "failures" attribute attached: data.table symbol error columns. CSV collected, read back aggregate OHLCV trades_to_ohlcv().
code: coinbase_backfill_trades(   symbols = c("BTC-USD", "ETH-USD"),   from = lubridate::as_datetime("2026-05-01", tz = "UTC"),   to = lubridate::as_datetime("2026-05-30", tz = "UTC"),   file = "trades.csv" )  # Resume an interrupted backfill -- just run the same call again. It reads the # existing CSV and continues each symbol from its last stored trade.

text: CoinbaseTrading class places, previews, edits, cancels, queries orders fills. endpoints require credentials.

title: Getting Started with coinbase
text: Orders carry order_configuration: one-key named list whose key names detailed order type. Common shapes:
code: # Market buy of $10 (quote-denominated) market_cfg <- list(market_market_ioc = list(quote_size = "10"))  # Limit buy of 0.001 BTC at 50000, good-till-cancelled limit_cfg <- list(   limit_limit_gtc = list(base_size = "0.001", limit_price = "50000") )

title: Getting Started with coinbase
text: preview_order() validates order without placing — executes nothing. returns estimated order_total, commission_total, sizes, best_bid/best_ask, slippage, validation errs, preview_id. Always preview submitting live order: preview_order() also accepts optional leverage, margin_type ("CROSS" "ISOLATED"), retail_portfolio_id arguments.
code: preview <- trading$preview_order(   product_id = "BTC-USD",   side = "BUY",   order_configuration = market_cfg ) preview[, .(order_total, commission_total, base_size, best_ask, errs)] #>    order_total commission_total base_size best_ask   errs #>          <num>            <num>     <num>    <num> <char> #> 1:       10.06             0.06  0.000135 74101.53   <NA>

code: order <- trading$add_order(   product_id = "BTC-USD",   side = "BUY",   order_configuration = limit_cfg ) order[, .(success, order_id, product_id, side)] #>    success                             order_id product_id   side #>     <lgcl>                               <char>     <char> <char> #> 1:    TRUE 1111aaaa-2222-bbbb-3333-cccccccccccc    BTC-USD    BUY

title: API Wrapper to Coinbase Advanced Trade
text: R API wrapper Coinbase Advanced Trade API supporting synchronous asynchronous (promise based) operations. Provides R6 classes market data, spot trading, account management, US futures (CFM), helpers tick--OHLCV aggregation.

title: API Wrapper to Coinbase Advanced Trade
text: software provided educational research purposes. Trading cryptocurrency carries substantial risk. solely responsible orders placed package. Use order preview methods (execute nothing) placing live orders.

title: API Wrapper to Coinbase Advanced Trade
text: data.table everywhere, list columns. Every method returns flat data.table; nested API objects flattened scalar columns. Sync async. Every method works modes. async = TRUE returns [promise][promises::promise]; otherwise results returned directly. single sync/async branch point. Exact money values. Prices, sizes, amounts transmitted full precision (never rounded scientific notation). Two hosts. Authenticated trading/account endpoints use Advanced Trade host (api.coinbase.com); deep public market data uses Exchange host (api.exchange.coinbase.com).

title: API Wrapper to Coinbase Advanced Trade
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("dereckscompany/coinbase")

title: API Wrapper to Coinbase Advanced Trade
text: Create API credentials https://www.coinbase.com/settings/api (download JSON name privateKey). Store environment variables .Renviron — PEM newlines escaped \n single line (see .Renviron.example): Load get_api_keys() (reads two environment variables default): Public market data needs credentials.
code:
COINBASE_API_KEY_NAME="organizations/<org-uuid>/apiKeys/<key-uuid>" COINBASE_API_PRIVATE_KEY="-----BEGIN EC PRIVATE KEY-----
...
-----END EC PRIVATE KEY-----
" box::use(coinbase[ get_api_keys ])  keys <- get_api_keys()

title: CoinbaseBase: Abstract Base Class for Coinbase API Clients — CoinbaseBase
text: async parameter controls execution mode API methods: async = FALSE (default): methods return results directly. async = TRUE: methods return promises::promise objects resolve types. Async mode requires promises package (Suggests). Consume promises coro::async() await() promises::(); drive event loop script use (optional) later package, e.g. (!later::loop_empty()) later::run_now().

title: CoinbaseBase: Abstract Base Class for Coinbase API Clients — CoinbaseBase
text: Coinbase splits across two hosts. Authenticated trading account endpoints live Advanced Trade host (get_base_url(), https://api.coinbase.com); public market-data endpoints deep history live Exchange host (get_exchange_base_url(), https://api.exchange.coinbase.com). Subclasses select host per request via base_url argument private$.request().

title: coinbase 0.0.1
text: Initial release: R wrapper Coinbase Advanced Trade API, supporting synchronous asynchronous (promise-based) operations.

title: coinbase 0.0.1
text: Market data (CoinbaseMarketData, public, auth): products, OHLCV candles, tick trades, order book (levels 1-3), ticker, server time Coinbase Exchange host. Deep tick history: CoinbaseMarketData$get_trades_history() standalone coinbase_backfill_trades() walk trades endpoint back product’s inception, incremental CSV writes resume. Aggregate ticks OHLCV timeframe trades_to_ohlcv(). Account (CoinbaseAccount): accounts/balances (cursor-paginated), fee tier, portfolios, key permissions. Trading (CoinbaseTrading): place, preview (dry run), edit, cancel, query orders fills. US futures / CFM (CoinbaseFutures): balance summary, positions, margin settings, cash sweeps short leg pairs strategies. Futures orders placed shared order endpoint futures product.

title: coinbase 0.0.1
text: Every public method returns data.table list columns. Authentication uses per-request ES256 (EC) EdDSA (Ed25519) JWT; uri claim excludes query string. Credentials come COINBASE_API_KEY_NAME / COINBASE_API_PRIVATE_KEY environment variables. requests flow single funnel (coinbase_build_request()) one sync/async branch point. Inputs validated assert package; times handled UTC via lubridate. Money values transmitted exact precision.
